sourceMovie.title: Disclosure
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Disclosure for the same reason it broke through in 1994: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Barry Levinson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Disclosure clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Confessions of a Dangerous Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Disclosure clicked for you, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Disclosure?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Confessions of a Dangerous Mind, I Spit on Your Grave—each shares DNA with Disclosure while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Disclosure?
faq.answer: Disclosure (1994) is best described as Thriller, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Disclosure worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
